2015-10-25.abstract: Seven people were missing after an inflatable dinghy carrying dozens of migrants from Turkey hit rocks near the island of Lesbos, Greek authorities said.

2015-10-26.title: Jimmy Morales Is Elected New President in Guatemala
2015-10-26.abstract: Running on the slogan “Not corrupt, nor a thief,” Mr. Morales benefited from his image as a political outsider after an anticorruption movement brought down President Otto Pérez Molina.

2015-10-26.title: 5 Britons Die in Sinking Near Tofino, British Columbia
2015-10-26.abstract: The British foreign secretary confirmed the nationalities of those who died after a whale-watching boat sank.

2015-10-26.title: E.U. and Balkan Leaders Discuss Migrants
2015-10-26.abstract: European Union and Balkan leaders agreed late on Sunday on measures to slow the movement of migrants whose flight from war and poverty has overwhelmed border guards across the continent.

2015-10-26.title: Earthquake Strikes Afghanistan and Pakistan, Multiplying War’s Woes
2015-10-26.abstract: At least 208 were reported killed, buildings collapsed and people fled into the streets after a 7.5 magnitude quake roiled sections of both countries.

2015-10-26.title: Argentine Candidates React to Tight Vote
2015-10-26.abstract: Voters on Sunday sent the presidential race in Argentina to a runoff. Here’s how the leading candidates reacted to the results.

2015-10-27.title: Challenging Chinese Claims, U.S. Sends Warship Near Artificial Island Chain
2015-10-27.abstract: The maneuver by the United States Navy is a direct challenge to China’s assertion that the artificial islands it has built in the South China Sea are a part of its territory.

2015-10-27.title: Belgian Authorities See No Terror Link in Car Crash at Army Barracks
2015-10-27.abstract: A masked man who rammed his car into an army commando barracks set off panic in southern Belgium, but the defense minister said there were no reason to believe that terrorism was involved.

2015-10-27.title: Unelected Chamber in Britain Delays Cameron’s Spending Cuts
2015-10-27.abstract: The House of Lords voted to delay the latest round of spending cuts proposed by Prime Minister David Cameron, which involve decreasing a cash benefit for people in low-paying jobs.

2015-10-27.title: Bangladeshi Officials Say 4 Arrested in Killing of Italian Aid Worker Were Not With ISIS
2015-10-27.abstract: The authorities dismissed claims that the Islamic State was behind a fatal shooting in Dhaka last month, saying the motive was to “keep the government under pressure.”

2015-10-27.title: Earthquake Interrupts Afghan Broadcast
2015-10-27.abstract: A TV presenter in Afghanistan was forced to leave his desk during a live broadcast on Monday as an earthquake of magnitude 7.5 struck the country.

2015-10-28.title: Stakes Rise in Standoff Between Catalan Separatists and Spanish Government
2015-10-28.abstract: Catalonia’s main separatist parties on Tuesday announced a new push for independence. The plan was swiftly denounced by Spain’s prime minister, Mariano Rajoy.

2015-10-28.title: Balkans Reel as Number of Migrants Hits Record
2015-10-28.abstract: Fresh fighting in Syria and growing fears of border closings are pushing record numbers to undertake a treacherous trek.

2015-10-28.title: Iran Is Invited to Join U.S., Russia and Europe for Talks on Syria’s Future
2015-10-28.abstract: If Iran agrees to attend the meeting in Vienna this week, it will be the first time Secretary of State John Kerry will enter formal negotiations with Tehran since the nuclear accord reached in July.

2015-10-28.title: Mexico Moves to Scale Back a Successful Tax on Soda
2015-10-28.abstract: Two years ago, the Mexican government imposed a tax of about 10 percent on sugary drinks. Now, legislators are trying to roll it back, angering public health experts.

2015-10-28.title: Philippine Leader Rules Out Resettlement
2015-10-28.abstract: President Benigno S. Aquino III of the Philippines said on Tuesday that his government is studying an Australian proposal to send refugees to his country, but he said they could not settle there.

2015-10-29.title: North Korea Is Accused of Abusing Workers
2015-10-29.abstract: A special United Nations investigator said that much of the earnings of more than 50,000 North Korean workers were taken by their government.

2015-10-29.title: Venezuelan Prosecutor Ties Nicolás Maduro to Arrests
2015-10-29.abstract: A prosecutor who fled to the United States said an official told him that the leader of Venezuela had ordered the arrests of several dissidents, including the opposition politician Leopoldo López.

2015-10-29.title: Violence Creating Refugee Crisis, a U.N. Commissioner Warns
2015-10-29.abstract: The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees said Wednesday that a refugee crisis was looming in Central and North America as people flee unchecked gang violence.

2015-10-29.title: Turkish Journalists Report Police Raid on Their Own Offices
2015-10-29.abstract: Reporters in Istanbul covered the raid on their offices as the government seized control of a media group critical of the ruling party.

2015-10-29.title: India Wants to Ban Birth Surrogacy for Foreigners
2015-10-29.abstract: The latest development in a recent push to regulate an industry that has been booming in India, with one study measuring it at more than $400 million.

2015-10-30.title: Mexico: Sugary-Drinks Tax Is Left Intact
2015-10-30.abstract: Mexico’s congress blocked an attempt to roll back part of a tax on soft drinks Thursday, responding to an uproar from public health advocates.

2015-10-30.title: Iran Is Said to Detain Another Iranian-American
2015-10-30.abstract: Siamak Namazi, a consultant who has advocated better relations between the United States and Iran, was arrested in Tehran around Oct. 15, according to people close to him.

2015-10-30.title: Experts Weigh Likely Impacts of China’s ‘One Child’ Reversal
2015-10-30.abstract: Some authorities are convinced that what will drive future prosperity is not population, but investment in technology, education and health care.

2015-10-30.title: As More Children Drown, Volunteers on Lesbos Say Rescues Are Left Largely to Them
2015-10-30.abstract: Volunteers on the Greek island of Lesbos lashed out at European governments and aid organizations, saying they are allowing migrants to suffer and die.

2015-10-30.title: South Korea: Russian Jets Approach U.S. Carrier
2015-10-30.abstract: The nuclear-powered aircraft carrier Ronald Reagan was taking part in a military exercise with South Korea when a pair of Russian bombers flew within one nautical mile of the ship.

2015-10-31.title: Deadly Fire Roars Through Romanian Nightclub
2015-10-31.abstract: More than two dozen people were killed and scores were injured in the fire that broke out during a rock concert in Bucharest.

2015-10-31.title: Cyclone Chapala, Gathering Strength, Heads Toward Oman and Yemen
2015-10-31.abstract: The system packed sustained winds of almost 150 miles per hour and threatened to bring dangerous torrential rains to two of the most arid countries in the world.

2015-10-31.title: Russian Airliner Crashes in Egypt, Killing 224
2015-10-31.abstract: The Kogalymavia flight from the Egyptian resort of Sharm el-Sheikh to St. Petersburg crashed shortly after takeoff, officials said.

2015-10-31.title: German Village of 102 Braces for 750 Asylum Seekers
2015-10-31.abstract: Sumte has become a showcase of the extreme pressures bearing down on Germany, and at least one neo-Nazi welcomes the news, if not the refugees.

2015-10-31.title: Battle Heats Up Over Exports of Surveillance Technology
2015-10-31.abstract: Silicon Valley is resisting federal plans to increase regulations on exports of “dual use” technology like servers and software used in social media that can also be used for spying.
